items.title: CVE-2026-35616: Fortinet FortiClient EMS Auth Bypass (CVSS 9.1) Actively Exploited
items.summary: Fortinet released emergency patches for CVE-2026-35616, a CVSS 9.1 improper access control flaw in FortiClient EMS that allows unauthenticated attackers to bypass authentication via the management API and escalate privileges. Exploitation in the wild was confirmed at time of disclosure.
items.why: An unauthenticated auth bypass in endpoint management software grants attackers control over every managed endpoint across an enterprise. Patch immediately — exploitation is already active.

items.title: CISA Adds CVE-2026-31431 Linux Kernel LPE to Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog
items.summary: CISA added CVE-2026-31431 (CVSS 7.8) to its KEV catalog — a local privilege escalation flaw in the Linux kernel that allows an unprivileged local user to obtain full root access. Federal agencies face a mandatory remediation deadline.
items.why: Linux underpins the vast majority of cloud and server infrastructure. A reliable LPE-to-root primitive is a critical post-exploitation stepping stone; container escapes and cloud lateral movement both become trivial with root on the host.

items.title: APT28 Weaponizes Microsoft Office Zero-Day CVE-2026-21509 Within 24 Hours Against NATO-Adjacent Governments
items.summary: Trellix Research documented APT28 (Fancy Bear) converting CVE-2026-21509, a Microsoft Office zero-day, into a weaponized spear-phishing payload within 24 hours of public disclosure. A concentrated 72-hour campaign (Jan 28–30 2026) targeted defense ministries, logistics operators, and diplomatic entities across nine Eastern European nations using cloud-hosted C2 infrastructure.
items.why: Sub-24-hour zero-day weaponization by a nation-state against NATO-adjacent defense entities signals a dangerous acceleration in offensive timelines — defenders can no longer rely on a patch window before exploitation begins.

items.title: Iran-Linked APT Screening Serpens Deploys 6 New RAT Variants Across 5 Countries Amid Regional Escalation
items.summary: Palo Alto Networks Unit 42 tracked Iran-nexus threat actor Screening Serpens rapidly expanding operations after February 2026 regional conflict escalation, deploying six new remote access trojan variants split across two new malware families — MiniUpdate and MiniJunk V2 — against targets in five countries.
items.why: A sudden proliferation of distinct custom malware families in under two months indicates the actor has expanded tooling resources and operational tempo significantly. Defenders in the Middle East and allied regions must update detections for these new families immediately.

items.title: CrowdStrike 2026 Global Threat Report: AI-Enabled Attacks Up 89%, Fastest Breakout Time Now 27 Seconds
items.summary: CrowdStrike's 2026 Global Threat Report reveals AI-enabled cyberattacks surged 89% year-over-year. Average adversary breakout time fell to 29 minutes with the fastest recorded lateral movement at just 27 seconds. Adversaries are also actively abusing GenAI tools — injecting malicious prompts into enterprise AI systems at 90+ organizations.
items.why: A 27-second breakout time eliminates any meaningful human-in-the-loop incident response window. Organizations must shift to automated, real-time containment and assume AI systems themselves are a new attack surface requiring prompt-injection defenses.
